vision_report.compositional_truth_check: Registry vs. Reality: P1 (Yellow Pot) appears empty/soil-only. P2/P4 (Black Pot) contains a single, struggling seedling. P3 (Black Pot) contains the Pothos specimen and the white rabbit anchor.

inventory_reconciliation.P2/P4: Systemic Loss (Original specimens absent; single sprout remains)

plant_audit.P1: Empty soil. No signs of germination or life.
plant_audit.P2/P4: Single, thin, chlorotic sprout. Appears to be a remnant or incidental growth rather than the registered Mexican Mint/Silver Guest.
plant_audit.P3: Pothos leaf remains turgid. Rabbit anchor is stable.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark and moist across all pots.
biome_observations.debris: Significant presence of crushed eggshells in P2/P4, likely an intentional calcium/pH intervention.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Clean, no significant biological spread outside of pots.

temporal_deltas.T-4_to_T-2: Gradual decline of the sprout in P2/P4; leaf tissue loss observed.
temporal_deltas.T-1: Extreme low-light/underexposed capture; visual confirmation of plant health impossible.
temporal_deltas.CURRENT: Sprout in P2/P4 shows slight postural shift (wilting/collapse) compared to EARLIEST image.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: P3 is stable. P1 is non-viable. P2/P4 is in critical decline; the remaining sprout lacks the vigor of a healthy specimen and is likely failing due to lack of light or root-zone issues.
vision_report.anomalies: Presence of eggshell fragments in P2/P4 is a non-registry intervention. The 'String of Nickels' and 'Mexican Mint' are effectively absent.
vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is currently dominated by a single, struggling sprout in the P2/P4 vessel. The Pothos in P3 remains the only robust occupant. The yellow pot (P1) is devoid of life. The intervention of eggshells suggests an attempt to amend soil chemistry, but the lack of progress in the P2/P4 sprout indicates a failure to thrive.
